The black finish and stainless steel construction look sharp out of the box, but black powder-coat exteriors are more susceptible to fading and chipping under prolonged UV and heat exposure than brushed stainless. At 44,000 BTU across four burners, the per-burner output is adequate rather than exceptional — buyers who prioritize extreme searing heat should note that some competitors push higher BTU counts. It's also an island-format unit, so it requires dedicated outdoor space and isn't suited to compact patios or balconies.

Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue

Monument's 4+2 burner cabinet grill earns its perfect 5/5 score across 71 reviews, making it one of the most consistently praised options here. The combination of four main burners plus two infrared side sear burners is a genuine cooking advantage — infrared searing runs significantly hotter than standard burners and produces the kind of crust you'd normally need a dedicated sear station to achieve. The Clearview® lid lets you monitor food without lifting and venting heat, and the built-in thermometer adds basic heat management without requiring separate tools. At $519, it's the most affordable full-featured grill on this list.

This is a propane-only unit as listed, which matters for buyers planning around a natural gas line. The blue color option is uncommon in the outdoor grill category and won't suit every patio aesthetic. Cabinet-style propane grills of this price point also tend to use lighter-gauge steel in non-cooking components, so longevity under year-round exposure depends heavily on storage and cover habits. Buyers in harsh climates should factor in a quality grill cover. Best Gas Grills Outdoor (2026).

Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head, 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill, Natural Gas Convertible, Heavy Duty 304 Stainless Steel 40,000BTUs BBQ Island Outdoor Kitchen

This Brand-Man built-in head is purpose-designed for outdoor kitchen installations, earning a 5/5 rating from 72 reviewers. The use of 304 stainless steel — a marine-grade alloy with strong corrosion resistance — is a meaningful spec distinction at the $889.99 price point, and it matters for longevity in humid or coastal environments. At 40,000 BTU across four burners in a 30-inch head, the output density is solid, and the natural gas convertibility means it integrates cleanly with a permanent gas line without requiring a separate conversion kit purchase. Best Gas Outdoor Grills (2026).

As a built-in head only, it requires an existing outdoor kitchen island or a custom enclosure — buyers who don't already have that infrastructure will face significant additional costs before this grill is usable. The 30-inch width is on the compact end for a four-burner configuration, which translates to tighter cooking zones. It's also propane-primary with a conversion option, so buyers who want a native natural gas unit should confirm conversion kit availability and compatibility before purchasing. Best Outdoor Gas Grills (2026).

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas

The Hygrill STD Series targets buyers who want a native natural gas built-in without the propane-conversion compromise. Its 5/5 rating across 41 reviews reflects strong satisfaction for a purpose-built outdoor kitchen component. The "heavy duty" designation and stainless steel construction suggest a grill head built for long-term installation rather than seasonal use, and at $1,129.99, it sits in a tier where build quality expectations are appropriately high. For outdoor kitchens designed around a permanent natural gas supply, this is one of the most direct-fit options available. Best Outdoor Grills Gas (2026).

Three burners is the most limited cooking capacity in this roundup, which constrains output for large gatherings. The price — $1,129.99 for a three-burner head — is high relative to competitors offering four burners at similar or lower cost, so the premium is justified only if natural gas native operation and build quality are the primary criteria. Like all built-in heads, it requires an existing island enclosure, adding cost and planning time for buyers starting from scratch. The smaller review count (41) compared to other five-star entries here means the rating has less statistical weight.

Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs

The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle is the only flat-top option in this lineup, making it the right choice for outdoor kitchens that need smash burgers, breakfast foods, stir-fry, and other cooking styles that a traditional grate simply can't handle. At 34,000 BTU across two burners, it delivers adequate heat for a 28-inch flat surface, and the stainless steel construction with a lid makes it a clean, permanent addition to a BBQ island. A 4.5/5 rating from 20 reviews shows early positive reception. Native natural gas operation means no conversion hassle for built-in setups. outdoor gas grills sale (2026).

A griddle cannot replace a grill — there's no open flame, no grate marks, and no wood-smoke transfer, so buyers who want traditional BBQ output need a separate grill alongside this unit. At $1,109.99 for a two-burner griddle head, the per-BTU cost is the highest in this group, and the 20-review sample is too small to draw firm conclusions about long-term durability. It's a specialized tool, not a primary grill, and should be budgeted as an addition to an outdoor kitchen rather than its centerpiece unless flat-top cooking is the explicit priority. For more options purpose-built for full outdoor kitchen setups, see the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) guide.

Buying guide

BTU output sets the ceiling on heat and recovery speed, but the number alone doesn't tell the full story — cooking surface area matters just as much. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner pairs 48,000 BTU across its main burners with 864 sq. in. of cooking space, which keeps BTU-per-square-inch density reasonable. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Grill Head delivers 40,000 BTUs on a 30-inch platform, a tighter ratio that translates to faster searing temperatures. As a rule, anything below roughly 80–90 BTU per square inch will struggle to reach and hold sear-level heat in cold or windy conditions.

Fuel flexibility separates versatile units from locked-in ones. Several grills here — the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Island and the Brand-Man Built-In Head — are explicitly LPG/NG convertible, meaning you're not committed to one fuel source at install time. Grills like the Hygrill STD Series and the Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle are natural gas native, so confirm your utility hookup before purchasing. If you're building a new outdoor kitchen and haven't run a gas line yet, convertible models give you more flexibility during construction. grills outdoor (2026).

Build material determines longevity under weather exposure. The Brand-Man Built-In Head specifies heavy-duty 304 stainless steel — the marine-grade alloy that resists rust better than the more common 430 stainless used in budget units. The Hygrill STD Series also markets itself as heavy-duty stainless construction. Porcelain-coated grates, as found on the Tytus Fresno, add non-stick convenience but can chip over time; solid stainless grates clean harder but last longer with less care.

Island integration versus freestanding setup is a decision that shapes your entire purchase path. Built-in heads like the Hygrill STD Series, the Brand-Man 30-Inch Head, and the Stanbroil griddle drop into existing masonry or prefab frames and don't include cabinetry — you supply the surround. Complete island stations like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and the Tytus Fresno ship with storage cabinets and prep surfaces already integrated, which saves fabrication cost but limits customization. For buyers linking this purchase to a larger outdoor kitchen build, a built-in head typically offers cleaner long-term results; for buyers who want a plug-and-cook setup, a complete island station is the faster path.

Related guides

Building a permanent setup? The best gas grills for outdoor kitchens breaks down drop-in and built-in options suited for countertop installations. For a broader look at fuel-specific picks, the best outdoor grills running on natural gas covers freestanding and island-style models side by side.

Frequently asked questions

What is a reasonable budget for a quality gas grill in 2026?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a well-built freestanding gas grill with multiple burners and a stainless steel cabinet. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner at $519 sits near the entry point for cabinet-style grills with infrared side sear burners. Serious built-in grill heads and island setups run $800–$1,400, as seen with the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island ($799.99), the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In ($1,129.99), and the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island ($1,399.99). Budget under $500 typically means compromises in grate quality, BTU output, or cabinet durability.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head 30-Inch or the Hygrill STD Series — is designed to drop into a pre-built outdoor kitchen island or counter cutout. It has no legs or cart and relies on the surrounding structure for support and storage. A freestanding grill ships as a complete unit with its own cabinet or cart, such as the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island. If you are building an outdoor kitchen from scratch and want a custom look, a grill head is the cleaner choice. If you want something ready to use immediately, a freestanding island grill is more practical.

Can I convert a propane gas grill to natural gas?

Many grills on this list support dual-fuel or LPG/NG conversion. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head is also natural gas convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island is listed as propane or natural gas. Conversion typically requires a separate conversion kit and adjustment of the orifices — confirm kit availability with the manufacturer before purchase. The Hygrill STD Series is listed specifically as a natural gas model. Always verify that the conversion maintains the stated BTU ratings and does not void the warranty. Best Outdoor Grills (2026).

How much cooking surface do I need for a family of four to six people?

A primary cooking area of 400–500 square inches handles four to six people comfortably for most grilling scenarios. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island leads this list at 864 sq. in. of total cooking surface, which is more than adequate for large gatherings. For a standard family cookout, the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner and the Brand-Man built-in options offer enough primary grate space without excessive footprint. If you regularly cook for crowds of eight or more, prioritize grills with 600+ sq. in. or a dedicated warming rack in addition to the primary grate.

Which gas grill has the best user ratings among the options listed?

Three grills on this list carry a 5/5 rating: the Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner (71 reviews), the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head 30-Inch (72 reviews), and the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In (41 reviews). Among those, the Monument and Brand-Man built-in head have the highest review counts, giving those ratings stronger statistical weight. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island scores 4.7/5 across 113 reviews — the largest review pool of any product listed — making it one of the most broadly validated options for buyers who want consensus-backed confidence. outdoor gas grills (2026).

Is a flat top griddle a good alternative to a traditional gas grill for outdoor kitchens?

A flat top griddle excels at breakfast foods, smash burgers, stir-fry, and anything that benefits from a large, even cooking surface and the ability to retain drippings. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle delivers 34,000 BTUs across two burners and is designed to drop into an outdoor kitchen island like a traditional built-in grill head. What it cannot do is impart direct flame char or grill marks. Many serious outdoor kitchen builds include both a grill and a griddle side by side. For buyers who cook varied menus, the Stanbroil makes a strong complement to any of the built-in grill heads above rather than a straight replacement. The included lid and stainless steel construction support permanent island integration, and native natural gas operation eliminates conversion steps. Buying guide and further reading

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast a grill recovers heat between loads, but raw numbers only mean something relative to cooking area. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU main-burner output across 864 sq. in. of cooking space — a reasonable ratio for a large island build. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island puts out 44,000 BTU over a more compact footprint, which translates to faster surface temperatures for everyday cooks. If the station will see high-volume use, prioritize BTU-per-square-inch density over total BTU alone. Best Outdoor Kitchen Gas Grills (2026).

Burner count and configuration matter more than many buyers expect. A dedicated side burner — present on both the Tytus Fresno (12,000 BTU) and the Brand-Man island station — keeps sauces and sides off the main grate without monopolizing primary cooking real estate. For pure flat-top work, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Natural Gas Griddle offers a 2-burner, 34,000 BTU setup specifically optimized for flat-surface cooking rather than grate grilling, which is a fundamentally different use case. Match the burner layout to how the station will actually be used, not just to the highest number on the spec sheet. outdoor kitchens with charcoal grills (2026).

Fuel flexibility and construction grade are the two specs most likely to affect long-term ownership. Several options here — including the Brand-Man built-in head and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner — are LPG/NG convertible, which matters if the installation site doesn't have a permanent gas line yet or if the setup might move. On materials, the Brand-Man Built-In Head and Hygrill STD Series both specify 304 stainless steel, a meaningful distinction over lesser grades that corrode faster under outdoor exposure and high heat cycling. Porcelain-coated grates, as found on the Tytus Fresno, are easier to clean but require more careful handling than raw cast iron or stainless alternatives. Best Outdoor Grills (2026).

Storage and prep infrastructure often gets underweighted at purchase. Enclosed storage cabinets — standard on the Tytus Fresno, Brand-Man island, and others — keep propane tanks, tools, and accessories sheltered from weather. The Brand-Man 4-Burner island adds a fold-out prep table, which is genuinely useful in kitchens where counter space is tight. Budget for these features deliberately rather than treating them as extras, since retrofitting cabinetry to an existing built-in is expensive. Best Charcoal Grills For Outdoor Kitchens (2026).

The broader roundup at Best Barbeque Grills Natural Gas (2026) is worth consulting if the choice between natural gas and propane is still open, and Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) goes further into freestanding versus built-in trade-offs for natural gas buyers.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a decent outdoor kitchen barbecue grill?

The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner cabinet-style grill comes in at $519 and earns a 5/5 rating across 71 reviews, making it the lowest entry point among quality options here. For a full island station with storage cabinets and a side burner, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island at $799.99 is the next step up. Expect to spend at least $1,100–$1,400 for a built-in grill head rated for permanent outdoor kitchen installation in stainless steel. Best Electric Outdoor Barbecue Grills (2026).

Can these grills run on both propane and natural gas?

Most models in this category are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head are both explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island also supports propane or natural gas. The Hygrill STD Series Built-In Grill Head is listed as natural gas only, so confirm the fuel type before purchasing if you need propane flexibility. Best Outdoor Bbq Gas Grills (2026).

What is the difference between an island grill and a built-in grill head?

An island grill — like the Tytus Fresno or the Brand-Man 4-Burner island station — arrives as a complete unit with storage cabinets, a countertop frame, and sometimes a fold-out prep table. A built-in grill head, such as the Brand-Man 30-Inch or the Hygrill STD Series, is just the cooking unit itself, designed to drop into a custom masonry or modular outdoor kitchen frame you already own or are building. Built-in heads give more design flexibility but require additional construction cost. read the full breakdown.

How much BTU output do I actually need for outdoor grilling?

For a standard family cook, 40,000–48,000 BTU across four to five main burners is more than sufficient. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. The Brand-Man 4-Burner island puts out 44,000 BTU. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head reaches 40,000 BTU. Higher BTU ratings matter most for fast preheat and high-heat searing; they become less important for low-and-slow cooking where you want precise low-end control.

Which grill is best suited for a permanent outdoor kitchen installation?

The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In Grill Head and the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head are purpose-built for permanent drop-in installation. Both use heavy-duty 304 stainless steel construction and are designed to integrate flush with countertop surfaces. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle is a strong companion or alternative for flat-top cooking in the same built-in format, rated at 34,000 BTU with a lid. For buyers who want a natural gas line run directly to the unit, all three of these built-in options support that configuration. Best Top Rated Outdoor Gas Grills (2026).

Is a flat-top griddle worth including in an outdoor kitchen alongside a grill?

A dedicated flat-top adds genuine cooking versatility — smash burgers, breakfast items, stir-fry, and delicate fish all cook better on a griddle than over open flame. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle at $1,109.99 is the only purpose-built griddle option in this lineup. At 34,000 BTU across two burners and 28 inches wide, it fits standard outdoor kitchen module widths and pairs cleanly next to a built-in grill head without requiring a separate fuel connection if you are already running natural gas. commercial outdoor gas grills (2026).

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output matters, but only in context. Raw BTU numbers mean little without knowing the cooking area they're heating. By contrast, the Brand-Man Island Station runs 44,000 BTU and includes a fold-out prep table, making it a stronger fit when prep space matters more than raw firepower. For griddle-forward cooking, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Flat Top delivers 34,000 BTU across two burners on a flat stainless surface — a completely different tool than a grate-style grill, not a downgrade.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality outdoor built-in barbecue grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a built-in or island-style grill with meaningful cooking area and durable construction. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner Cabinet Style comes in at $519 and represents the realistic floor for a stainless steel unit with infrared sear burners and a built-in thermometer. Below that price point, frame quality and burner longevity tend to drop noticeably. Purpose-built drop-in grill heads — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head at $889.99 or the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99 — cost more because they're engineered specifically for permanent island installation.

What's the difference between a built-in grill head and a grill island?

A built-in grill head (sometimes called a drop-in head) is the cooking unit only — it drops into a cutout in an existing outdoor kitchen counter or masonry island. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner are both sold this way. A grill island is a self-contained unit that ships with its own frame, storage cabinets, and often a side burner, so no separate outdoor kitchen structure is needed. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island are complete island packages. If you're building a custom outdoor kitchen from scratch, a grill head gives more design flexibility; if you want a standalone setup, an island is faster to install.

Can these grills run on both natural gas and propane?

Most models on this list are dual-fuel convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is listed as propane or natural gas. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is labeled LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is described as natural gas convertible. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle is listed specifically for natural gas. Always confirm which conversion hardware is included versus sold separately before purchasing, and check local code requirements for permanent natural gas line connections. check this comparison.

How much cooking surface do built-in grills typically offer?

Cooking area on built-in and island grills ranges considerably by form factor. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island provides 864 sq. in. of total cooking surface — one of the largest figures in this category. Drop-in grill heads typically offer less total area but concentrate heat more efficiently. When sizing a grill for a permanent outdoor kitchen, factor in how many people you regularly cook for: roughly 72 sq. in. per person is a workable guideline, meaning a six-person household benefits from at least 430 sq. in. of primary cooking area. built in grills outdoor (2026).

Which built-in grill has the most BTUs for high-heat searing?

Among the products listed here, the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner delivers 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island outputs 44,000 BTU, and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head produces 40,000 BTU. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle runs at 34,000 BTU across two burners. For searing steaks or high-heat cooking, raw BTU output matters less than BTU-per-square-inch — a smaller grill at 40,000 BTU can run hotter per unit area than a larger grill at 48,000 BTU spread across more grates.

Are built-in barbecue grills suitable for a covered patio or pergola?

Built-in grills can be installed under a covered structure, but ventilation is the critical factor. Gas combustion requires adequate airflow, and smoke needs a clear exit path. Most grill and appliance manufacturers require a minimum clearance between the grill and any overhead structure — commonly 36 to 60 inches — and recommend a dedicated outdoor hood or ventilation system for fully covered installations. Check the specific clearance requirements in the product documentation before finalizing your outdoor kitchen design, and consult local building codes, which vary by jurisdiction. Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality built-in outdoor grill?

Expect to spend at least $800–$900 for a built-in grill head made from 304 stainless steel with meaningful BTU output. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head at $889.99 represents a realistic entry point — it delivers 40,000 BTUs across four burners in heavy-gauge 304 stainless. Below that price range, material quality and heat output tend to drop noticeably. Complete grill islands with storage cabinets start around $800 (Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island at $799.99), though those are freestanding units rather than pure drop-in heads. Best Built In Outdoor Grills (2026).

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a grill island?

A built-in grill head is a drop-in cooking unit designed to be set into a countertop or masonry surround you already own or are building. A grill island is a self-contained station that includes the grill, storage cabinets, and sometimes a side burner or fold-out prep table — everything pre-assembled. The Hygrill STD Series and Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head are drop-in heads; the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island and Tytus Fresno are full islands. If you are building a custom outdoor kitchen, a grill head gives more design flexibility. If you want a turnkey setup, an island is faster to deploy.

Can these built-in grills run on natural gas or do they require propane?

Most models on this page are dual-fuel or natively natural gas. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head is explicitly natural gas convertible and ships with conversion hardware. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is labeled LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno runs on either propane or natural gas. The Hygrill STD Series is listed as a natural gas unit. If your outdoor kitchen is plumbed for a gas line, verify whether the model ships ready for natural gas or requires a conversion kit, since that affects installation time and cost.

How do I size a built-in grill for my outdoor kitchen?

Burner count and cooking area are the two key numbers. A three-burner unit like the Hygrill STD Series suits smaller islands and households cooking for four to six people regularly. Four-burner units — the Brand-Man 30-Inch head at 30 inches wide — handle larger gatherings without dominating a mid-size island. The Tytus Fresno steps up to five burners and 864 sq. in. of cooking space, which is appropriate for frequent entertaining or large families. As a rule, measure your cutout opening first, then match it to the listed drop-in dimensions of the grill head before purchasing.

Is a flat-top griddle a viable built-in alternative to a traditional grate grill?

Yes, and it serves a different cooking style rather than being a lesser option. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle puts 34,000 BTUs across a flat stainless cooking surface, making it purpose-built for smash burgers, breakfast foods, seared fish, and vegetables that fall through standard grates. At $1,109.99 it is priced comparably to grill heads in this category. Many outdoor kitchens incorporate both a traditional grill head and a built-in griddle side by side for full versatility. The Stanbroil ships with a lid and is designed specifically for BBQ island installation. gas grills for outdoor kitchens (2026).

Which built-in grill is best for a large outdoor kitchen with heavy use?

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is the most capable unit in this lineup for high-volume cooking — 48,000 BTU main burners, a separate 12,000 BTU side burner, and 864 sq. in. of total cooking surface give it significant throughput. At $1,399.99 it is the highest-priced option here but includes storage cabinets and is semi-portable if your setup needs to move. For a pure drop-in head in a large masonry island, the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner at 40,000 BTUs with 72 five-star reviews offers proven reliability at $889.99. Buying guide and further reading

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast a built-in grill can sear, recover temperature after a cold piece of meat hits the grates, and hold a steady zone across a large cook surface. The range across this lineup runs from 34,000 BTUs on the Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle up to 48,000 BTUs on the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill (main burners only, plus an additional 12,000-BTU side burner). For most residential outdoor kitchens, 40,000–44,000 BTUs across four burners hits the practical sweet spot — enough power to run independent heat zones without the infrastructure demands of a commercial unit.

Grill head format matters as much as raw heat. A drop-in or built-in head like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head or the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In Grill integrates flush into a masonry or prefab island counter, keeping the finished look clean and the footprint fixed. An island station — such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno — bundles the cabinet structure, storage, and sometimes a fold-out prep table into a single unit, which simplifies installation but limits customization. If you want a flat cooking surface for smash burgers, eggs, and vegetables alongside traditional grate cooking, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle is the only purpose-built flat-top option in this group at 34,000 BTUs across two burners.

Frequently asked questions

What is the price floor for a quality built-in outdoor kitchen grill?

Expect to spend at least $500–$800 for a built-in unit with genuine stainless steel construction and enough BTUs to sear properly. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner Cabinet Style comes in at $519 and delivers a verified 5/5 rating across 71 reviews, making it the accessible entry point. Step up to the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island at $799.99 for a more complete station with storage cabinets and a fold-out prep table. Dedicated grill heads designed for drop-in installations — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head at $889.99 or the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99 — cost more because they are engineered specifically for permanent outdoor kitchen frames rather than freestanding use. outdoor grills built in (2026).

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and an island grill?

A grill head is just the cooking unit — it drops into a cutout in a masonry island, concrete counter, or modular frame you already own or are having built. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series are both grill heads. An island grill arrives as a self-contained station with cabinetry, side burners, and prep surfaces already integrated. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island fall into this category. If you are building a custom outdoor kitchen from scratch, a grill head gives more design flexibility. If you want a plug-and-play outdoor kitchen without masonry work, an island grill is faster to set up.

Can these grills run on natural gas, or are they propane only?

Most units on this list are dual-fuel or explicitly natural-gas compatible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island is labeled LPG/NG convertible. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head ships propane-ready but is natural-gas convertible. The Hygrill STD Series Built-In Head is listed as natural gas. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is offered in both propane and natural gas configurations. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle is specifically a natural gas unit. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner is a propane model. Always confirm with the seller which conversion hardware is included, since some conversions require a separately purchased orifice kit.

How many BTUs do I actually need for an outdoor kitchen grill?

For a typical 4-burner outdoor grill cooking for 6–10 people, 40,000–50,000 BTUs across the main burners is a practical benchmark. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU on its main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner — the strongest main-burner output in this group. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island produces 44,000 BTU total, while the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head puts out 40,000 BTU. For flat-top cooking specifically, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle's 34,000 BTU across two burners is appropriate given the different heat-distribution method a griddle uses versus open grates.

Is 304 stainless steel worth the premium over standard stainless?

Yes, especially in coastal or high-humidity environments. 304 stainless contains 18% chromium and 8% nickel, giving it meaningfully better corrosion resistance than the 430-grade stainless used in many budget grills. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head explicitly calls out heavy-duty 304 stainless steel construction. For a permanent outdoor kitchen installation where replacing a corroded grill head means cutting it out of a frame, the durability premium is worth it. Best Built-In Outdoor Kitchen Grills (2026).

What size grill do I need for an outdoor kitchen island?

Sizing depends on both your cooking volume and your island's cutout dimensions. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle suits compact islands or those who prioritize flat-top cooking over high-capacity grilling. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is a common mid-size footprint that fits most standard island cutout specifications. For larger entertaining, the Tytus Fresno's 864 sq. in. total cooking area across 5 burners handles serious volume. Measure your island cutout before ordering any built-in head, and cross-reference the product's listed installation dimensions — not just the cooking surface size. At the other end, the Brand-Man 4-Burner island station puts out 44,000 BTU, which is sufficient for most backyard sessions but leaves less headroom for large-group cooking. As a rule of thumb, plan for roughly 100 BTU per square inch of grate space if you want consistent heat across the full surface.

Fuel flexibility matters more than many buyers realize before purchasing. Every grill on this list either runs natively on natural gas or is LP/NG convertible, which means you can tie into a permanent gas line rather than managing propane tanks. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series, for instance, are explicitly built for natural gas outdoor kitchen installs — their drop-in format assumes a fixed fuel supply. If your setup isn't finalized yet, a convertible unit like the Brand-Man 4-Burner island (LPG/NG convertible) buys you flexibility while the infrastructure catches up.

OUR TOP PICK

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill – 864 sq. in. VIEW ON AMAZON →

Grate material and lid design separate grills that produce a proper sear from those that merely cook food. Porcelain-coated grates, as on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat well and resist sticking without requiring the seasoning regimen cast iron demands. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner adds a Clearview® lid and infrared side sear burners — useful if you want steakhouse-level crust without a separate searing station. Built-in thermometers, present on the Monument and several others here, are a convenience feature but no substitute for a probe; use them as a zone reference, not a doneness guide.

For buyers building a full outdoor kitchen rather than buying a standalone unit, the decisions around grill selection intersect with countertop layout, cabinetry, and gas line routing — topics covered in depth on the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen guide. If natural gas compatibility is the primary filter for your search, the best outdoor grills natural gas roundup narrows the field specifically to NG-ready units with direct comparisons across price tiers. Both pages are worth reading before finalizing a purchase if your setup involves a permanent gas connection.

Frequently asked questions

What is a reasonable budget for a heavy-duty outdoor barbecue grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a cabinet-style propane or natural gas grill with meaningful cooking area and durable grates. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner sits at $519 and represents a solid entry point with infrared side sear burners and a clearview lid. For built-in island heads in 304 stainless steel, budget climbs toward $889–$1,130 (Brand-Man 30-Inch Head, Hygrill STD Series). Full island systems with storage cabinets and side burners run $800–$1,400, as seen with the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island at $799.99 and the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island at $1,399.99.

Which grills can run on both propane and natural gas?

Several models on this list are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is described as natural gas convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island is sold as "Propane or Natural Gas." If you plan to connect to a dedicated gas line — common in outdoor kitchens — confirm the conversion kit is included or available before purchasing, since some manufacturers ship one configuration and sell the conversion separately.

How much cooking surface do these grills offer?

Cooking area varies significantly across this lineup. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner tops the group at 864 sq. in., making it the strongest choice for large gatherings. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head are mid-range island options whose specs support four burners at 44,000 BTU and 40,000 BTU respectively, though exact square-inch figures are not listed for those models. For a flat-top alternative, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle delivers a dedicated 28-inch cooking surface with 34,000 BTUs across two burners — purpose-built for smash burgers, breakfast cooks, and stir-fry rather than traditional grill marks.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch or the Hygrill STD Series — is a drop-in unit designed to be set into a countertop or masonry outdoor kitchen frame. It ships without cabinetry, so the surrounding structure is your responsibility. A freestanding island grill, such as the Tytus Fresno or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island, arrives as a complete station with integrated storage cabinets and a frame, ready to position on a patio without additional construction. Built-in heads typically suit homeowners building a permanent outdoor kitchen; island grills are better for buyers who want a finished look without custom fabrication. Best Outdoor Barbecue Grills Built In (2026).

Are high BTU ratings a reliable indicator of grilling performance?

BTU output tells you how much fuel a burner can consume, not how efficiently heat is transferred to food. A grill with quality cast iron or porcelain-coated grates and a well-sealed lid — like the Tytus Fresno's porcelain grates — can outperform a higher-BTU competitor with poor heat retention. That said, BTU numbers do matter for recovery time after opening the lid and for achieving sear temperatures quickly. The Tytus Fresno's 48,000 BTU main burner plus 12,000 BTU side burner is a strong combination for large-volume cooking. For built-in heads, the Hygrill STD Series and Brand-Man 30-Inch both offer substantial output suited to permanent outdoor kitchen setups.

Is a natural gas griddle worth considering alongside a traditional grill?

Yes, if your cooking style extends beyond burgers and steaks. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle at $1,109.99 brings a flat-top cooking surface to an outdoor kitchen, handling eggs, pancakes, sautéed vegetables, and smash-style burgers in ways a grated grill cannot. At 34,000 BTUs across two burners and built in 304 stainless steel with a lid, it fits flush into an island cutout. Buyers with a large outdoor kitchen often pair a built-in grill head with a separate griddle station. For more options in this category, see the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen guide.

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast you can cook and how well the grill recovers heat after a cold piece of meat hits the grates. For a commercial-grade outdoor barbecue, look for at least 40,000 BTU across the main burners. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner — meaningful headroom for high-volume cooking. The Brand-Man Built-In Head hits 40,000 BTU from four burners, while the Brand-Man Island Station runs 44,000 BTU with a side burner included. Anything below that range will struggle to maintain sear temperatures when the grill is fully loaded.

Cooking surface area determines how many portions you can run simultaneously. The Tytus Fresno's 864 sq. in. is the largest in this lineup and suits catering-scale output. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle takes a different approach — a flat-top format with 34,000 BTU across two burners is better suited to breakfast service or smash burgers than traditional grate grilling, so match the surface type to your menu before buying. Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum BTU output worth considering for a commercial outdoor barbecue grill?

For commercial or heavy-duty outdoor use, look for at least 40,000 BTU across the main burners. The Brand-Man Built-In Head hits exactly 40,000 BTU from four burners, while the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner delivers 48,000 BTU on the main grate plus a separate 12,000 BTU side burner — a meaningful step up when cooking for large groups. Lower BTU figures work fine for residential weekend grilling but fall short under sustained commercial demand.

Can these grills run on both propane and natural gas?

Several models in this category are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Grill Island is explicitly LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man Built-In Head ships as propane with natural gas conversion capability. The Tytus Fresno is listed as propane or natural gas from the factory. The Hygrill STD Series and Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are natural-gas-dedicated units. Always confirm which conversion kit is included versus sold separately before purchasing, especially for permanent outdoor kitchen installations.

What is the price floor for a quality commercial outdoor barbecue grill?

The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner at $519 represents the entry point among the options reviewed here, offering stainless steel cabinet construction, infrared side sear burners, and a Clearview lid. Island-style grill stations with storage cabinets start closer to $800 — the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island comes in at $799.99. Purpose-built or heavy-duty stainless units like the Hygrill STD Series ($1,129.99) and the Tytus Fresno ($1,399.99) sit at the upper end and are better suited to near-daily commercial use.

Is a built-in grill head or a freestanding island grill better for a permanent outdoor kitchen?

Built-in grill heads integrate flush into a custom surround and generally deliver a cleaner, more permanent look. The Brand-Man Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series are designed specifically for drop-in installation into an outdoor kitchen island. Freestanding island stations like the Tytus Fresno and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island include their own storage cabinets and can function as standalone units — useful if the outdoor kitchen is still under construction or if portability matters. The Tytus Fresno is specifically noted as semi-portable.

What cooking surface area do commercial outdoor grills typically offer?

The Tytus Fresno leads the group at 864 sq. in. of total cooking area across five burners. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle provides a flat-top cooking surface spanning two burners at 34,000 BTU — a different format suited to high-volume breakfast or smash-burger service rather than traditional grate grilling. For outdoor kitchens where versatility matters, pairing a grill head with a separate griddle unit is a common commercial approach. See the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen guide for layout recommendations.

What grate material holds up best under commercial use?

Porcelain-coated cast iron grates, like those on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat well and resist rust if the coating stays intact. Heavy-gauge 304 stainless steel grates — the standard on the Brand-Man Built-In Head — resist corrosion more reliably under daily use and are easier to restore if scratched. For high-frequency commercial environments where cleaning is aggressive and covers may be skipped, 304 stainless is the more durable long-term choice. Porcelain grates are excellent when maintained but can chip under metal utensils, which shortens their service life.

Frequently asked questions

What is the cheapest outdoor grill available at Costco right now?

The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grill comes in at $519, making it the lowest entry point among the options currently listed. It still delivers real substance — 4 main burners plus 2 infrared side sear burners, a cabinet-style stainless steel body, and a Clearview lid with built-in thermometer. For buyers who want a freestanding grill without crossing into the $800+ island territory, it's the strongest value on this list.

Which grills can convert between propane (LPG) and natural gas?

Several models here support dual-fuel flexibility. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island ($799.99) is explicitly LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island ($1,399.99) ships as propane or natural gas. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head ($889.99) is also natural gas convertible. If you're planning a permanent outdoor kitchen plumbed to a gas line — or you want the option to switch later — these three are the ones to prioritize. The Hygrill STD Series and Stanbroil Griddle are sold as dedicated natural gas units.

Are Costco grills worth buying compared to specialty grill retailers?

Costco's advantage is competitive pricing on island-style and built-in units that typically carry higher margins at specialty stores. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island at $799.99 with a 4.7/5 rating across 113 reviews is a strong data point — that volume of reviews at that score is hard to dismiss. The trade-off is that selection rotates seasonally and support for niche replacement parts can be limited. For buyers building a permanent outdoor kitchen, it's worth cross-shopping against dedicated outdoor kitchen retailers, but for value on fully configured island grills, Costco competes seriously. outdoor grills (2026).

What's the difference between a built-in grill head and a full grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Head ($889.99) or the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Head ($1,129.99) — is the cooking unit only. It drops into a custom-built or pre-fabricated outdoor kitchen frame. A full grill island — like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner ($1,399.99) or Brand-Man 4-Burner Island ($799.99) — comes with the cabinet structure, storage, and countertop already assembled. Built-in heads give more design control but require additional infrastructure investment. Islands are faster to deploy and better suited for buyers who want a complete setup without a construction project.

Is the Stanbroil griddle a replacement for a traditional grill, or a complement to one?

The Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle ($1,109.99) is a flat-top unit, not a grate grill — so it won't produce grill marks or direct-flame char. It excels at smash burgers, breakfast items, stir-fry, and anything that benefits from broad, even surface contact. Most buyers treating this as a primary cooker should expect to pair it with a grate-based grill for full versatility. In a built-out outdoor kitchen, it functions as a powerful complement rather than a standalone replacement.

How much BTU do you actually need for an outdoor grill?

BTU figures require context — cooking surface area matters as much as raw output. A useful benchmark is 80–100 BTU per square inch of primary cooking area. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU across 864 sq. in. of cooking space, which works out to roughly 55 BTU per sq. in. — adequate for most grilling, though not high-intensity searing territory on every zone. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island puts 44,000 BTU into its cooking area. Higher BTU matters most for searing at high heat quickly; for low-and-slow or standard grilling, output in the 40,000–50,000 range is sufficient for family-sized cooks. The Monument's infrared side sear burners add targeted high-heat capability without needing a higher total BTU rating.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a serious outdoor natural gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a freestanding cabinet-style grill with meaningful BTU output and stainless steel construction. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner at $519 represents the realistic entry point for a grill with infrared sear burners, a clearview lid, and a built-in thermometer. Below that price, compromises in materials and heat output become significant. For built-in grill heads designed for permanent outdoor kitchen installation, the floor rises to roughly $890–$1,130, as seen with the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head at $889.99 and the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner or the Hygrill STD Series — is a drop-in unit designed to be set into a permanent countertop or masonry structure. It has no cabinet frame of its own. A freestanding grill island — like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island — arrives as a complete station with integrated storage cabinets, a finished exterior, and legs or casters. Built-in heads offer more design flexibility for custom outdoor kitchens; islands are ready to use without construction work.

Which grills here convert between propane and natural gas?

Several options on this list are dual-fuel convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is listed as propane or natural gas compatible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is explicitly LPG/NG convertible. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is also natural gas convertible from propane. The Hygrill STD Series Built-In is listed specifically for natural gas. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner is a propane unit. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle is a dedicated natural gas model. Always verify which conversion hardware is included versus sold separately before purchasing.

How much cooking area do these grills offer?

The largest primary cooking surface in this group belongs to the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner at 864 sq. in. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head deliver 44,000 BTU and 40,000 BTU respectively, though primary cooking area dimensions are not specified in their listings. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle provides a flat-top cooking surface on a 28-inch platform. For outdoor kitchen builds where total square footage matters, the Tytus Fresno's stated 864 sq. in. is the clearest benchmark in this set.

Are any of these grills rated highly enough to trust for a first-time buyer?

Reviewer confidence is strong across the board here. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island carries a 4.7/5 rating across 113 reviews — the largest review sample in this group and a reliable signal of consistent satisfaction. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series both hold 5/5 ratings with 72 and 41 reviews respectively. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner also sits at 5/5 with 71 reviews. First-time buyers who want peer-validated performance have several well-reviewed options at different price points.

Is a side burner worth the extra cost on an outdoor grill?

A side burner adds genuine utility if the outdoor kitchen is meant to handle complete meals — sautéing vegetables, warming sauces, or boiling water without running inside. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner includes a 12,000 BTU side burner alongside its 48,000 BTU main output. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island also includes a side burner. For occasional weekend grilling focused on proteins, the side burner is a convenience rather than a necessity. For anyone replacing an indoor kitchen during warm months, it earns its place.

Frequently asked questions

Does Walmart actually sell electric outdoor grills, or are most options gas-powered?

Walmart's outdoor grill selection skews heavily toward propane and natural gas models. Dedicated electric outdoor grills exist on the site but represent a smaller subset. If a strictly electric unit is the goal, filtering by fuel type on Walmart.com narrows the field quickly. The guides in this silo — including Best Electric Outdoor Grills At Walmart and Best Electric Outdoor Grills Walmart — surface the strongest current options in that category.

What differentiates an electric outdoor grill from an electric outdoor barbecue or griddle?

The terms overlap but point to slightly different cooking styles. An electric outdoor grill uses raised grates and direct radiant heat to produce grill marks and some char. An electric outdoor barbecue typically implies a larger cooking chamber designed for slower, indirect heat. A flat-top electric griddle, like the outdoor grills electric category covers, trades grill marks for a broad, even cooking surface suited to smash burgers, eggs, and stir-fry. Knowing which cook style you need before shopping saves time.

Are electric outdoor grills powerful enough for serious grilling?

Most residential electric outdoor grills top out between 1,500 and 2,000 watts, which translates to roughly 5,000–7,000 BTU equivalents — enough for burgers, chicken, and vegetables but short of the searing power a high-BTU gas burner delivers. For heavy-duty searing on a patio, gas models with infrared burners hold a clear advantage. Electric units compensate with precise temperature control, no open flame, and suitability for balconies or HOA-restricted spaces where gas is prohibited. The electric BBQ grills outdoor guide breaks down which wattage tiers handle which tasks.

What should a buyer look for in a heavy-duty electric outdoor grill?

Prioritize cast-iron or thick porcelain-coated grates, which retain heat better and hold up to outdoor conditions. A tight-fitting lid matters for convection cooking and weather protection. Look for a grounded, weatherproof power cord rated for outdoor use, a built-in thermometer or digital controller, and grease management that drains away from the heating element. Stainless steel housing resists rust far better than painted steel over multiple seasons. The electric outdoor barbecue grills guide covers construction quality in more detail.

How do the subcategory pages in this silo differ from one another?

Each page targets a specific buyer context. The electric outdoor grills at Walmart and electric outdoor grills Walmart pages focus on what is currently available and stocked through Walmart's platform. The outdoor grills electric page broadens to category-wide buying advice. The electric BBQ grills outdoor page emphasizes barbecue-style cooking, while the electric outdoor barbecue grills page goes deeper on build quality and long-term ownership. Start with whichever matches your immediate question, then cross-reference the others for specs and comparisons.

Is it worth buying an electric outdoor grill at Walmart versus a specialty retailer?

For entry-level to mid-range electric grills, Walmart's pricing is typically competitive, and in-store pickup eliminates shipping damage risk — a real concern with heavy grills. Specialty retailers may carry a wider selection of commercial-grade units and offer more knowledgeable staff for built-in outdoor kitchen installations. For a standalone electric grill under $300, Walmart is a reasonable first stop. For a built-in island setup or anything requiring custom BTU output or precise cutout dimensions, a dedicated outdoor kitchen dealer adds value worth the premium. outdoor electric bbq grills (2026).

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output is the most cited spec in this category, but raw heat numbers only matter in context. A 2-burner griddle like the Stanbroil 28-Inch pulls 34,000 BTUs across a flat cooking surface — efficient for smash burgers and breakfast cooks. Step up to the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island and you're looking at 48,000 BTUs on the main grates plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner, which makes sense only if you're cooking for a crowd regularly and need simultaneous zones. For most backyard setups, a 4-burner head in the 40,000–44,000 BTU range — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch at 40,000 BTUs or the Brand-Man Island at 44,000 BTUs — hits the sweet spot between fuel efficiency and usable heat.

Construction grade separates weekend grills from built-ins designed to survive years of outdoor exposure. Look for 304 stainless steel rather than a generic "stainless" designation — the Brand-Man 4-Burner Head specifies 304 explicitly, which resists chloride corrosion far better than 430-grade alternatives. Grate material matters too: porcelain-coated grates on the Tytus Fresno retain heat evenly and resist rust, while bare stainless rod grates demand more maintenance but tolerate higher direct heat without flaking. For built-in installations, the grill head's mounting flange and cabinet compatibility are non-negotiable — measure your island cutout before ordering. electric outdoor grills (2026).

Frequently asked questions

What is the price floor for a heavy-duty outdoor grill worth buying?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a freestanding cabinet-style unit with meaningful BTU output and stainless steel construction. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner at $519 sits at that entry point with infrared side sear burners and a clearview lid. Built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchen islands start closer to $890, as seen with the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner at $889.99. Below $500 the build quality and heat output typically drop off sharply for outdoor use. Best Electric Grills Outdoor (2026).

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner or the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner — is a drop-in cooking unit designed to be set into a custom outdoor kitchen counter or BBQ island that you build or buy separately. A freestanding grill island, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill, arrives with the cabinet structure, storage, and prep space already integrated, so it is ready to use without additional construction.

How much BTU output do I actually need for outdoor grilling?

For everyday backyard cooking, 40,000–50,000 BTUs across the main burners is a solid target. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner hits 40,000 BTU, and the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner delivers 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. Higher BTUs matter most when you are searing at high heat or cooking for large groups — but raw BTU numbers mean little if the grill has poor heat retention or thin grates, so look at grate material alongside the spec.

Can these grills run on both natural gas and propane?

Several models here offer dual-fuel flexibility. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner is explicitly listed as natural gas convertible, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is rated for both LPG and NG. The Tytus Fresno is available in propane or natural gas configuration. The Hygrill STD Series and Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are listed specifically as natural gas units. Always confirm the fuel type before purchasing — conversion kits are sometimes included, but not universally.

Which grill is best for an outdoor kitchen island build?

The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner at $1,129.99 and the Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle at $1,109.99 are both purpose-built grill heads for outdoor kitchen integration. If you want the highest-rated option by review score, the Hygrill carries a perfect 5/5 from 41 reviews. If a flat-top griddle surface suits your cooking style, the Stanbroil's 34,000 BTU 2-burner layout with a lid is the stronger pick in that category.

Are these grills available for pickup or delivery through Walmart?

All products listed on this page are shown as in stock. Walmart's availability for large outdoor grills typically includes home delivery, with select items eligible for store pickup depending on location. Because stock and fulfillment options change frequently, check the current product page on Walmart.com to confirm your specific delivery or pickup options before ordering. Frequently asked questions

What is the price floor for a serious outdoor grill setup?

Expect to spend at least $519 for a full cabinet-style station like the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner, which includes infrared side sear burners and a clearview lid. Built-in grill heads start around $889.99 for the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner, and complete island stations with storage climb to $799.99–$1,399.99 depending on size and BTU output. Budget models under $500 rarely offer the stainless steel construction or BTU ratings needed for consistent heavy-duty outdoor cooking. Best Grills Electric Outdoor (2026).

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series or the Brand-Man 30-Inch — is designed to drop into a custom outdoor kitchen surround or BBQ island you already own or are building. A freestanding grill island such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner arrives with its own cabinet base, storage, and prep surfaces, requiring no separate construction.

How do I choose between natural gas and propane?

Natural gas is better for permanent installations with a dedicated gas line — it eliminates tank swaps and typically costs less to run long-term. Propane suits setups where running a gas line is impractical. Several models here, including the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner and the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner, are convertible between NG and LPG, so you are not locked in at purchase.

How much cooking area do these grills actually provide?

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner leads this group with 864 sq. in. of total cooking space, backed by 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle focuses on flat-top cooking with two burners at 34,000 BTUs combined. For most households cooking for six to ten people, 400–600 sq. in. and 40,000+ BTUs is a practical benchmark.

Which model has the strongest buyer rating?

Three models in this lineup carry a perfect 5/5 score: the Hygrill STD Series (41 reviews), the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner (72 reviews), and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner (71 reviews). The Brand-Man and Monument models combine the highest review counts with perfect scores, giving those ratings more statistical weight than a five-star average from a handful of buyers.

Are there electric outdoor grills worth buying, or is gas the better choice for heavy-duty outdoor cooking?

For heavy-duty outdoor BBQ, gas grills consistently outperform electric in raw heat output, recovery speed, and the ability to create the high-temp searing zones serious grilling demands. Electric grills have their place — balconies with no gas access, HOA-restricted communities — but none of the products in this lineup are electric, reflecting the reality that buyers seeking a true outdoor BBQ station almost universally choose gas. For a broader look at electric options, see the Best Electric BBQ Grills Outdoor guide.

Frequently asked questions

What is the price floor for a heavy-duty outdoor grill island setup?

Expect to spend at least $799.99 for a full grill island station with storage. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island sits at that entry point and delivers 44,000 BTU, a side burner, fold-out prep table, and LPG/NG convertibility. Purpose-built built-in grill heads designed to drop into existing outdoor kitchens start around $889.99, as with the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head. Premium griddle inserts and heavy-duty 3-burner built-in heads run $1,109–$1,129, and full island systems with extensive cooking area push toward $1,399.99.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner or the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner — is a drop-in unit intended to be framed into an outdoor kitchen counter or island structure you already have or are building. A freestanding grill island, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill, arrives as a self-contained unit with cabinets, countertop, and grill already integrated. Freestanding islands are faster to set up; built-in heads offer more design flexibility for custom outdoor kitchens. Best Rated Outdoor Electric Grills (2026).

How much cooking area do these grills offer?

Cooking surface varies significantly across this category. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill leads with 864 sq. in. of total cooking area, which is well suited for large gatherings. Exact primary cooking surface specs are not published for every model listed here, so BTU output serves as a secondary sizing proxy — the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner, Brand-Man 4-Burner Island, and Brand-Man Built-In Head range from 40,000 to 48,000 BTU across their main burners, indicating full-size cooking grates rather than compact weekend units.

Which grills can run on both propane and natural gas?

Several models in this lineup are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is explicitly LPG/NG convertible. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is listed as propane with natural gas convertibility. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is sold as propane or natural gas. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle and the Hygrill STD Series are spec'd for natural gas. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner is a propane unit. Always confirm conversion kit inclusion before purchase if you plan to connect to a gas line. electric outdoor grills walmart (2026).

Which option makes the most sense for an outdoor kitchen remodel versus a standalone patio setup?

For an outdoor kitchen remodel where you are framing a counter or island structure, a built-in grill head gives the cleanest result. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head (304 stainless steel, 40,000 BTU) and the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built-In Grill are both designed specifically for that application. If you want a turnkey patio setup without construction work, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island at $799.99 or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill at $1,399.99 arrive ready to use with integrated storage and prep space.

Are any of these grills rated highly enough to justify the price premium?

Several carry strong buyer validation. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner holds a perfect 5/5 across 71 reviews at $519, which is a rare combination of volume and score. The Brand-Man Built-In Head also sits at 5/5 with 72 reviews at $889.99. The Hygrill STD Series Built-In Grill matches that perfect score with 41 reviews at $1,129.99. Buying guide and further reading

For households that entertain regularly or cook for more than four people, anything below 600 sq. in. will create bottlenecks. Island-style stations with side burners, like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island, extend that functional capacity without requiring a second appliance for sauces or sides. electric grills outdoor (2026).

BTU output matters, but only in context. Raw heat numbers are often cited without reference to cooking area, which makes them meaningless in isolation. The Tytus Fresno posts 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner — a ratio that produces genuine searing heat across a large surface without cold zones. The Brand-Man Built-In Head hits 40,000 BTU across four burners on a 30-inch frame, which is a reasonable density for a built-in format. Lower-BTU griddle setups like the Stanbroil 28-Inch (34,000 BTU, two-burner flat top) are calibrated for even, moderate heat across a flat cooking surface rather than high-flame searing, which is exactly what flat-top cooking requires.

Frequently asked questions

What is the price floor for a serious built-in outdoor grill head?

Expect to spend at least $800–$900 for a quality built-in grill head in heavy-gauge stainless steel. The Brand-Man 30-inch 4-Burner Built-In Head in 304 stainless steel starts at $889.99, which represents a realistic entry point for a unit that handles both propane and natural gas and delivers 40,000 BTUs. Below that price range, material quality and BTU output tend to drop noticeably.

Which option is best for a full outdoor kitchen island setup?

If the goal is a complete, ready-to-use island station rather than a built-in head, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island at $799.99 includes storage cabinets, a fold-out prep table, and a side burner in a single package. It also converts between LPG and natural gas, which matters if your utility setup changes. For a larger cooking footprint, the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island at $1,399.99 offers 864 sq. in. of cooking area plus a 12,000 BTU side burner and storage cabinets. outdoor grills (2026).

How do these gas island grills compare to electric indoor-outdoor grills for everyday use?

Gas island grills deliver higher sustained heat output — the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner unit includes infrared side sear burners, which electric grills cannot match for quick, high-heat searing. Electric grills trade raw BTU performance for convenience: no fuel management, easier apartment or balcony use, and simpler cleanup. For a dedicated outdoor kitchen with permanent installation, gas wins on performance. For flexible indoor-outdoor use without a gas line, electric is the practical choice. See the best electric outdoor grills at Walmart for a direct comparison.

Is a flat-top griddle insert worth considering over a traditional grill grate?

Yes, depending on what you cook most. The Stanbroil 28-inch 2-Burner Built-In Natural Gas Griddle at $1,109.99 provides a flat cooking surface ideal for smash burgers, breakfast items, and fish that would fall through traditional grates. At 34,000 BTUs across two burners, it heats evenly and fits standard BBQ island cutouts. If you want both options, some outdoor kitchen builds pair a griddle module with a separate grill head in adjacent cutouts. Best Outdoor Grills (2026).

What BTU output should buyers prioritize for a family-sized outdoor kitchen?

For a family of four to six, a main burner output of 40,000–48,000 BTUs across four or five burners is a solid target. The Tytus Fresno tops this range at 48,000 BTU main plus a 12,000 BTU side burner, while the Brand-Man Built-In Head delivers 40,000 BTUs in a 30-inch footprint. More BTUs is not always better — even heat distribution across the grates matters as much as peak output, which is where porcelain-coated or heavy stainless grates earn their keep.

Can these grills convert between propane and natural gas?

Several models in this category are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island and the Brand-Man 30-inch Built-In Head are both explicitly LPG/NG convertible, which is useful if you plan to run a permanent natural gas line to your outdoor kitchen later. The Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built-In Grill is listed specifically as natural gas. Always confirm the conversion kit is included or available before purchase, as some manufacturers charge separately for it. outdoor electric grills (2026).

BTU output is the first number to anchor any comparison, but raw heat alone doesn't tell the whole story. Construction grade separates weekend grills from long-term outdoor kitchen investments. 304 stainless steel — the spec called out explicitly on the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head 30-Inch — resists corrosion significantly better than 430-grade material, which matters in coastal and high-humidity climates. Cooking surface area follows directly from how many people you're feeding: the Tytus Fresno offers 864 sq. in. total, while a compact built-in head like the Hygrill STD Series is sized for island integration rather than standalone capacity. Match the footprint to your island cutout dimensions before anything else — a 28-inch griddle slot and a 30-inch grill head are not interchangeable. Best Electric Grills For Outdoor (2026).

OUR TOP PICK

The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas nails heavy-duty stainless steel construction built for island integration, a 5/5 rating across 41 verified buyers, and natural gas compatibility that eliminates tank swaps for permanent outdoor kitchen installs. $1129.99

VIEW ON AMAZON →

Fuel flexibility is worth checking even if you already know your hookup. Several options here — including the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island — are LPG/NG convertible, which preserves resale value and gives you a fallback if your gas line isn't yet run. Cabinet storage and fold-out prep surfaces sound like conveniences but become load-bearing features in compact outdoor kitchens where counter space is genuinely limited.

For shoppers comparing availability and pricing across retailers, the guides covering the best electric outdoor grills at Walmart and the broader roundup of top electric BBQ grills for outdoor use offer additional context on how these categories overlap — useful if you're still weighing gas versus electric before committing to an installation.

Frequently asked questions

What is the price floor for a heavy-duty outdoor grill at Home Depot?

Expect to spend at least $519 for a capable cabinet-style propane grill — the Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner sits at that entry point and still delivers infrared side sear burners and a clearview lid. Purpose-built outdoor kitchen grill heads and island packages start closer to $800–$900, with premium five-burner island systems reaching $1,399. electric outdoor grills (2026).

Which grill offers the most BTUs for high-heat searing?

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill leads the field at 48,000 BTU from the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner, totaling 60,000 BTU across the station. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is close behind at 44,000 BTU combined. If raw heat output is the priority, the Tytus is the clear choice.

Can I convert these grills from propane to natural gas?

Several models support dual-fuel use. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head (30-inch, 4-burner) is explicitly natural gas convertible, as is the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island. The Tytus Fresno also runs on either propane or natural gas. The Hygrill STD Series and Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are sold specifically as natural gas units, so confirm the fuel type before purchasing if you need LPG compatibility.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and an island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series or Brand-Man 30-inch 4-Burner Head — is a drop-in unit designed to be installed into an outdoor kitchen frame or BBQ island you already own or are building. An island grill, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island or Tytus Fresno, is a self-contained station that includes storage cabinets, prep surfaces, and sometimes a side burner, so no additional structure is required.

How much cooking surface do these grills provide?

The Tytus Fresno offers the largest total cooking area at 864 sq. in. across its five-burner layout. Built-in heads like the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle and the Hygrill STD Series are sized for integration and are narrower by design. For standalone station cooking — entertaining large groups or doing multiple proteins simultaneously — the Tytus or the Brand-Man island packages are the more practical picks.

Are electric outdoor grills a better fit than gas for certain buyers?

Electric grills suit balconies, HOA-restricted patios, or urban rooftops where open-flame grills are prohibited. They also require no fuel storage or line connection. If your outdoor space has no gas hookup and propane tank management is inconvenient, an electric model is a practical alternative. For comparable electric options across retailers, see the Best Electric Outdoor Grills At Walmart and Best Electric BBQ Grills Outdoor guides for side-by-side context.

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a full grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head or the Hygrill STD Series — is a drop-in cooking unit designed to be set into a countertop frame you already have or plan to build. A full grill island, such as the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island, comes with its own cabinet enclosure, storage, and sometimes a prep surface. If you are building a custom outdoor kitchen from scratch, a grill head gives more design flexibility. If you want a self-contained station without masonry or fabrication work, a complete island is the faster path. outdoor kitchen grills built-in (2026).

How much BTU output do I actually need for a backyard grill?

BTU output matters most relative to cooking surface area, not as a raw number. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU across 864 sq. in. of cooking space, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner island puts out 44,000 BTU on a smaller footprint — both are in a reasonable range for outdoor use where heat dissipates quickly. As a general benchmark, aim for roughly 80–100 BTU per square inch of primary cooking surface. Higher output is useful for searing; consistent heat distribution across burners matters more for everyday grilling. compare options here.

What should I look for in grill grates for outdoor kitchens?

Cast iron holds heat well but requires maintenance to prevent rust in outdoor environments. Porcelain-coated grates, used on the Tytus Fresno, resist rust and are easier to clean, though heavy use can chip the coating over time. Heavy-gauge 304 stainless steel grates, found on the Brand-Man Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series, are the most durable long-term choice for a permanent outdoor kitchen installation — they handle weather exposure without coatings that can degrade. Best Built In Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

Can these grills convert between propane and natural gas?

Several models on this page are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner island and the Brand-Man Built-In Head are both listed as LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno is available as propane or natural gas. If your outdoor kitchen is connected to a gas line, natural gas eliminates the need to swap tanks — a practical advantage for high-frequency use. Always verify that the conversion kit is included or available for your specific unit before purchase. Best Outdoor Kitchen Built-In Grills (2026).

Is a flat-top griddle a good addition to an outdoor kitchen island?

A flat-top griddle excels at foods that fall through grill grates — smash burgers, breakfast items, seafood, and vegetables. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Natural Gas Griddle at 34,000 BTU is designed specifically as a drop-in island component. Rather than replacing a grill, a built-in griddle is most useful as a second cooking zone alongside a primary burner grill, giving an outdoor kitchen genuine versatility without needing a separate appliance. Best Built-In Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

For a complete outdoor kitchen configuration with cabinetry and countertop integration, Best Outdoor Kitchen BBQ Grills and Best Outdoor Kitchen Barbecue Grills cover fully integrated setups. If you are dropping a grill head into an existing island or countertop, Best Outdoor Built-In BBQ Grills and Best Outdoor BBQ Grills Built In focus on that format.
